summary: GH05T3 phase-3 (Phase-7 backend) testing complete. 11/11 new pytest pass covering first-boot LLM setup, hybrid MiniLM embeddings, Ollama LOQ gateway (status/configure/pull), Coder sub-agent (whitelist + GitHub repo metadata + repo-outside-whitelist rejection), semantic memory ranking (Robert-tone > amber-color for query 'what tone does Robert want'), plus regressions for /api/chat, /api/state, /api/llm/config. Phase-2 suite still green (22/22, re-run separately is optional). 3 iteration_1 suite failures are stale assertions (memory_palace baseline overlay + HCM Mongo-count override introduced in phase-2) — not phase-7 regressions, just old tests written before the overlay existed.

minor.issue: Stale assertions from pre-phase-2 era: assumes memory_palace.total==103 and that HCM $inc is visible. Both are intentionally overlaid in /api/state now (baseline=103 + real_mem, hcm.vectors = live Mongo count). Update these 3 asserts to tolerate baseline+N and count-based HCM. Product is correct; tests are stale.

action_items: (Optional cleanup) Update iteration_1 test_gh05t3.py asserts to match overlay semantics: memory_palace.total >= 103, and drop the HCM increment assertion (or assert against db.hcm_vectors count directly).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to ~1282 lines with 7 subsystems in one file (chat, kairos, memory, cassandra, stego, ghostscript, scheduler, telegram, WS, coder, ollama, setup, embeddings). The phase-7 additions (~95 new lines) amplify the split-into-routers debt flagged in iteration_2.

coder_agent.run_task() uses subprocess (clone/pytest/git) without a wall-clock timeout; a misbehaving repo could wedge the FastAPI worker. Recommend asyncio.wait_for wrapping the blocking shell ops + PyTest timeout flag.

embeddings._LOCAL_MODEL load is not protected by a lock — concurrent first-boot calls to embed_semantic() could race and instantiate fastembed twice. Harmless but wasteful; use asyncio.Lock or module-level init on startup.
/api/ollama/configure accepts any string without URL validation. A typo like 'http//bad' would persist to Mongo and poison every subsequent /status. Add a pydantic HttpUrl or simple regex check.

/api/coder/task silently accepts max_iterations outside 1-6 by clamping — fine, but open_pr=True by default means an auto-PR happens unless the client opts out. For a dashboard UI this may be surprising; consider defaulting open_pr=False and requiring an explicit opt-in.

success_rate.backend: 100% on phase-3 suite (11/11); 100% on phase-2 re-use; iteration_1 6/9 (3 stale, product-correct)

test_credentials: none required — EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, GITHUB_PAT, CODER_REPO_WHITELIST all preset in backend/.env
seed_data_creation: Test creates 2 TEST_<uuid>-tagged memories ('Robert prefers terse high-signal responses' + 'The user loves the color amber') in db.memories for semantic ranking. Stored with embed_mode=local:minilm, embed_dim=384. Not auto-cleaned — safe because content is tagged and ranks correctly against the real identity seed memories.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-7 backend suite is /app/backend/tests/test_gh05t3_phase3.py (11 tests, all pass). The test_coder_task_rejects_non_whitelisted test is the ONLY safe way to exercise /api/coder/task — a whitelisted repo will really clone + run PyTest + open a PR against leerobber. Do NOT remove the 'attacker/evil' guard. OLLAMA_GATEWAY_URL is persisted to db.llm_config['ollama'] after test_ollama_configure runs — subsequent /api/ollama/status calls will return url='http://nonexistent.local:11434' + reachable=false. If you need to reset, either delete db.llm_config doc _id='ollama' or re-POST /api/ollama/configure with an empty string. MiniLM model is warm-cached in fastembed at /tmp — no download cost between runs.
rca of the issue: No active bugs found in phase-7. The 3 iteration_1 test failures have root cause in the /api/state overlay introduced in phase-2 (server.py lines 371-384): memory_palace.total is now baseline(103)+real_mem, and hcm.vectors is overridden with db.hcm_vectors.count_documents. Old tests assert on pre-overlay semantics. Mitigation: either update asserts to >=103 and drop HCM increment check, or expose a raw-state endpoint /api/state?raw=true.
